facts.text: San Castle sits on a ridge that once formed a 30‑foot‑high barrier island of sand and coquina from the Anastasia Formation.
North of San Castle lies the historic Boynton Mound complex, an indigenous site largely destroyed in the early 20th century for shell fill.
San Castle rests on the Atlantic Coastal Ridge, a relict shoreline and dune system linked to Pleistocene terraces that formed about 125,000 years ago.
The high elevation and well‑drained sands of San Castle support remnants of Florida scrub, one of the rarest ecosystems in Palm Beach County with less than 2% remaining.
San Castle enjoys a tropical monsoon climate, hot humid summers and warm drier winters, all moderated by the Gulf Stream just a mile offshore.
Since the 1980s, San Castle has evolved into a vibrant hub for Caribbean‑American culture, reflecting the diverse heritage of its residents across Palm Beach County.
